title: Understand: Equal Protection: African Americans
description: Check your understanding of how the Court's interpretation of the Equal Protection Clause has affected the lives of African Americans with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: The Fourteenth Amendment: Citizenship, Freedom, and Equality

assessment_items.question_content:
**What clause in the Fourteenth Amendment was used in *Brown v. Board of Education* (1954) to end school segregation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: allowed the practice of "separate but equal"
granted African Americans the right to vote

assessment_items.question_content:
**Writing for the majority in *Plessy v. Ferguson*, Justice Henry Billings Brown wrote that while the goal of the Fourteenth Amendment was to enforce equality before the law, the amendment also \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: permitted states to deny women voting rights
allowed for separate but equal facilities

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which reasons did Justice John Marshall Harlan give for dissenting in *Plessy v. Ferguson*? **


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: In the eye of the law, there is no superior, dominant class of citizens.
The Constitution is colorblind and does not take race into account.
The majority’s decision would prove as harmful as the *Dred Scott* ruling.
State governments, not the federal government, have the power to regulate segregation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which lawyer, who later became the first Black justice to serve on the Supreme Court, read Justice Harlan’s dissent in *Plessy* before making his own argument in *Brown v. Board of Education*?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**In the landmark ruling of *Brown v. Board of Education*, which chief justice concluded that, “in the field of education, the doctrine of ‘separate but equal’ has no place. Separate educational facilities are inherently unequal”?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which test does the Supreme Court use to decide if a law that requires a distinction based on race is constitutional?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**In which section of the Fourteenth Amendment can you find the Equal Protection Clause?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**What was the primary purpose of the Civil Rights Act of 1964?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To ban discrimination in public settings
To enshrine unenumerated rights into law
To give the federal government the power to enforce segregation

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did the Supreme Court say in its decision in *Loving v. Virginia* (1967)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Laws that banned interracial marriages violated the Fourteenth Amendment.
The right to marry was not an unenumerated right that states must protect.
Separate but equal facilities did not violate the Equal Protection Clause.
The Citizenship Clause guaranteed citizenship for any person born in the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In *Loving v. Virginia,* the Supreme Court concluded that the Virginia law banning interracial marriage violated which TWO clauses of the Fourteenth Amendment?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which statement best describes the Supreme Court’s ruling in *Students for Fair Admissions v. Harvard* (2023)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Race-conscious admissions policies violate the Equal Protection Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment.
Admissions policies that take gender into account violate the Equal Protection Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment.
Geographical preferences in student admissions do not violate the Equal Protection Clause  of the Fourteenth Amendment.
Racial quotas for admissions do not violate the Equal Protection Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment.
